User provided their resume. Name: Raphael Scott. Title: Technical Product Manager. Email: raphael.scott@gmail.com. Phone: 555-555-5555. LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/raphaelscott. Location: New York, NY. Summary: Technical Product Manager with IT background, consulting experience, proudest achievement: implemented automated process resulting in $20M profit increase. Professional experience: Technical Product Manager at Amazon (01/2019 - Ongoing, New York, NY) - created product roadmap resulting in $10M additional annual revenue, established methodology for partner communication, assembled task force for customer acquisition platform restructuring resulting in 118% increase in sales cycle. Technical Product Manager at Comcast (01/2017 - 01/2019, New York, NY) - led team launching automated process resulting in $20M profit increase, brought new partners expanding to 3 additional cities, collaborated on training program. IT Consultant at PwC (01/2015 - 01/2017, New York, NY) - trained Python engineers, implemented CRM system increasing sales revenue by 23%, streamlined assessment process cutting expenses by 14%. IT Business Analyst at Deloitte (01/2013 - 01/2015, New York, NY) - designed solutions reducing waiting/administrative times by 40%, maintained client relationships, saved average $250K per year for clients. Strengths: Analytical Thinking (track KPIs resulting in average 35% profit increase), Communication (engineering background allows collaboration with engineers), Problem Solving (led team solving high-priority problems for 3 consecutive years). Technical Skills: Scrum, Product Development, UX Design, Tableau, JIRA, Python, Kanban. Education: MBA from Stevens Institute of Technology (01/2008 - 01/2012, Hoboken, NJ), BS Industrial Engineering from UC Berkeley (01/2004 - 01/2008, Berkeley, CA). User requested rewrite to be more actionable and show capabilities better for C-Level executive review.

User provided Step 1 of a legal process: Check the NCAT Appeal Panel can decide the appeal. Section 32 of the Civil and Administrative Tribunal Act 2013 tells which NCAT decisions can be appealed to the Appeal Panel. Refer to NCAT Guideline 1 - Internal Appeals and the Act. If cannot appeal to NCAT Appeal Panel, may have right to appeal to NSW Supreme Court. Seek legal advice before considering appealing to NSW Supreme Court.

User provided Step 2 of a legal process: Consider legal representation. Before lodging an internal appeal, consider whether to present own case or have a lawyer/non-lawyer agent. Usually need to be present to give instructions. In an internal appeal, can be represented if able in original matter; otherwise need permission. Ask for permission when notified for 'call over'. Fill out Notice of Representation and submit to NCAT registry. If non-lawyer agent, sign before submission.
Notice of Representation by a Legal Practitioner or Agent

User provided Step 3 of a legal process: Complete the Notice of Appeal. Fill out Notice of Appeal form with: copy of orders and written reasons, details of NCAT decision being appealed, contact details of appellant and representative, contact details of respondents and their representatives, orders wanted changed, outline why Tribunal decision was wrong, orders Appeal Panel should make instead, copies of other documents. Also asked whether asking for leave to appeal; if unsure, answer 'yes' and give reasons.
